title: Lesson 27
paragraph: This lesson focuses on practical B1 vocabulary for everyday communication.

words.meaning: to respect or like someone because of what they do
words.example: I admire my sister because she works hard.

words.meaning: to say that something is true, especially something difficult
words.example: He will admit his mistake to the teacher.

words.meaning: to become visible or seem to be a certain way
words.japaneseMeaning: 現れる、〜のように見える
words.example: Dark clouds appear before the storm.

words.meaning: to fasten or connect one thing to another
words.example: Please attach the photo to your email.

words.meaning: to cook food in an oven
words.example: We bake bread every Saturday morning.

words.meaning: to be damaged by fire or heat
words.example: Do not burn the toast in the oven.

words.meaning: to run after someone or something
words.example: The dog likes to chase a ball in the park.

words.meaning: to go up something using your feet and often your hands
words.example: We climb the hill to see the town.

words.meaning: to find something for the first time
words.example: Scientists discover new facts about the ocean.
Students can earn extra money with a part-time job.
